rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Right To Homestead Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2026. Line 1.3. PLAINTIFF JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS Notice Of Hearing On Right To Homestead Exemption Status report. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CORE: SF LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2026. Line 4.1. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE - Fria R. Kermani is continued to January 15, 2026. rulings.Case Title: BHSD TPC PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. CORE: SF LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE - DAVID E. ROSS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2026. Line 4.3. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE - DAVID E. ROSS is continued to January 15, 2026. No later than January 8, 2026, moving party to file and serve (1) proof of service of the application and the notice of hearing of the application on the State Bar (CRC 9.40(c)); (2) supplemental declaration of someone with personal knowledge that the application fee to the State Bar was paid, confirming the date and the amount of the payment; and (3) supplemental declaration/verification of the applicant, verifying under penalty of perjury that the information stated in the verified application is true as of at least December 4, 2025. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2026. Line 4.2. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE - MARC E. KASOWITZ is continued to January 15, 2026. No later than January 8, 2026, moving party to file and serve (1) proof of service of the application and the notice of hearing of the application on the State Bar (CRC 9.40(c)); (2) supplemental declaration of someone with personal knowledge that the application fee to the State Bar was paid, confirming the date and the amount of the payment; and (3) supplemental declaration of the applicant, stating whether or not the previous application was granted (CRC 9.40(d)). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA GOUT VS. UNISON AGREEMENT CORP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL FOR PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 2, 2026. Line 5. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL FOR PRO HAC VICE is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005 and 1010.6. Verified petition and proofs of service are not executed under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California. (CCP 2015.5.) No proof of service on the State Bar of California. (CRC 9.40(c).) The Court also notes that the motion cites a non-existent California Rule of Court as its sole legal basis. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
